nodes.context: Australian organised crime group operating out of Merrylands, Western Sydney, involved in drug trafficking.
Dominant faction of the Brothers for Life organisation, involved in Sydney gangland war.
Organised crime group with chapters in Bankstown and Blacktown, involved in infighting.

aliases: Islamic State of Iraq and Syria

Allies of the Alameddine family, linked to alleged extremist activity.
Outlaw motorcycle club; Masood Zakaria was a former lieutenant.
Gang; Samer Marcus is a member who was subject to attempted murder.

Notorious family involved in 2016-2017 Sydney gang war.

Organisation of which Hamzy/Hamze family is the most dominant faction.
Outlaw motorcycle club; an associate was killed in the feud.
Criminal family involved in the Sydney underworld, allies of Hamzys.
Criminal group; Hamad Assad was an 'executioner' for them.

Islander gang used as muscle by Alameddines.

Subset within the Alameddine crime network, led by Asaad Alahmad.

Street gang, long-time ally of the Alameddine family, involved in tobacco war and other crimes.

edges.evidence_quote: Since October 2020, the Alameddines have grown to public notoriety in connection to an extended feud they became involved in with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family
edges.context: The Alameddines are in a feud with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: the Hamzy/Hamze crime family, the most dominant faction of the Brothers for Life organisation
edges.context: The Hamzy/Hamze crime family is a faction of Brothers for Life.

edges.evidence_quote: During legal proceedings, Talal Alameddine implied the network having a connection to the Islamic State (ISIS) militant group
edges.context: Talal Alameddine implied the network has a connection to ISIS.

edges.evidence_quote: Alameddine's long-time allies, the Hauochars
edges.context: The Hauochars are long-time allies of the Alameddine network.

edges.evidence_quote: Zakaria also used to be a lieutenant in the Comanchero Motorcycle Club under former sergeant-at-arms Tarek Zahed
edges.context: Masood Zakaria, a Brothers for Life member, was formerly a lieutenant in the Comanchero Motorcycle Club.

edges.evidence_quote: Talal Alameddine got into a brawl with fellow Goulburn Correctional Centre inmate Bassam Hamzy, founder of the Brothers for Life organisation.
edges.context: Talal Alameddine brawled with Bassam Hamzy, founder of Brothers for Life.

edges.evidence_quote: The war began when Safwan Charbaji, a relative of Steven Elmir, was shot to death at a smash-repair shop by Walid 'Wally' Ahmad in April 2016.
edges.context: The Elmir and Ahmad families were at war starting in 2016.

edges.evidence_quote: Since October 2020, the Alameddines have grown to public notoriety in connection to an extended feud they became involved in with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family
edges.context: The two groups are engaged in a violent feud.

edges.evidence_quote: The Comanchero Motorcycle Club, who were close allies and business partners of the Alameddines through Mohammad 'Almo' Alameddine
edges.context: Comancheros are close allies and business partners of the Alameddines.

edges.evidence_quote: Police sources stated that Vokai was viewed as the leader of the infamous Islander gang KVT, who have regularly been used as muscle by the Alameddines.
edges.context: KVT is used as muscle by the Alameddines.

edges.evidence_quote: This suggests that the Alameddines and Elmirs are still allies.
edges.context: The Alameddines and Elmirs are allies.

edges.evidence_quote: the Ahmads, close allies of the Hamzys
edges.context: The Ahmad family are close allies of the Hamzys.

edges.evidence_quote: Bilal Alameddine wrote a friendly letter to Mahmoud 'Brownie' Ahmad
edges.context: Bilal Alameddine wrote a friendly letter to Mahmoud Ahmad, suggesting a cooperative relationship.

edges.evidence_quote: The 2022 Comanchero–Ahmad skirmish
edges.context: The Comancheros and Ahmads were involved in a skirmish with shootings.

edges.evidence_quote: Alameddine/R4W member Bilal Mahfoud was shot at
edges.context: Bilal Mahfoud is a member of both Alameddine and R4W, indicating a structural link.

edges.evidence_quote: Ah-Cheung is an associate of the 'RFA' (Ready For Anything) street gang, which is strongly linked to the Mount Druitt street gang Onefour
edges.context: RFA is strongly linked to Onefour.

edges.evidence_quote: Since October 2020, the Alameddines have grown to public notoriety in connection to an extended feud they became involved in with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family, the most dominant faction of the Brothers for Life organisation, within the Sydney gangland war of the early 2020s.
edges.context: The Alameddines are in a feud with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The Daily Telegraph reported that Rafat Alameddine spent time as a younger man shadowing Bilal Haouchar, and learning the criminal trade under his guidance.
edges.context: Rafat Alameddine learned from Bilal Haouchar, indicating a mentorship relationship.

edges.evidence_quote: On 29 November 2024, associates of the Alameddine and KVT crime networks attempted to rob a tobacco delivery truck in Guildford.
edges.context: Associates of both networks jointly attempted a robbery.

edges.evidence_quote: The men arrested were part of a subset within the Alameddine crime network known as "R4W", or Ready for War.
edges.context: R4W is a subset of the Alameddine crime network.

edges.evidence_quote: In May 2025, The Daily Telegraph reported that in-fighting had allegedly began between the Alameddine family and the KVT street gang, their long-time ally.
edges.context: The Alameddine family and KVT were long-time allies but allegedly began feuding in 2025.
